content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Implemented the remaining prompt-contract remediation for bead `ralph-burning-9ni.6.1`. I replaced `FileSystem::prompt_hash()` with a stable SHA-256 digest so prompt drift/resume state no longer depends on `DefaultHasher`, and added a golden regression for the exact digest plus a `gitdir:` marker regression for live workspace path resolution. I also made `render_bead_task_prompt()` preserve fenced `AGENTS / Repo Guidance` blocks verbatim by making canonical-heading escaping fence-aware, while still escaping unfenced canonical heading lines. Finally, I stopped the affected test helpers from hardcoding `base_dir/.git/ralph-burning-live` by routing them through the production filesystem workspace-root helpers, so worktree/submodule-style gitdir resolution is exercised by the same code path as production.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the pending amendments in prompt hashing, AGENTS guidance rendering, and test helper workspace-path resolution to identify the exact production and regression-test call sites.
2. [Completed] Patch `src/adapters/fs.rs` to use a stable SHA-256 prompt digest, expose the production workspace-root helpers for external tests, and add deterministic regressions for digest stability and `gitdir:` marker resolution.
3. [Completed] Patch `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs` so AGENTS-guidance canonical-heading escaping is fence-aware, update the affected integration-test helpers to use production workspace-root helpers, add the fenced-guidance regression, and run focused formatting/tests.

## Validation Evidence

- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo fmt --all` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo fmt --all --check` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test prompt_hash_uses_stable_sha256_digest -- --nocapture` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test live_workspace_root_path_resolves_gitdir_marker_files -- --nocapture` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test render_bead_task_prompt_preserves_fenced_canonical_headings_inside_agents_guidance_verbatim -- --nocapture` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test render_bead_task_prompt -- --nocapture` successfully, including the new fenced-AGENTS-guidance regression alongside the existing canonical prompt rendering coverage.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test create_project_from_bead_context -- --nocapture` successfully after the hashing and AGENTS-guidance changes.

## Outstanding Risks

- I ran focused formatting and targeted regression coverage for the hashing, prompt-rendering, and workspace-helper paths, not the full repository test suite.
- Changing prompt hashes from the legacy 16-hex `DefaultHasher` output to SHA-256 will intentionally change stored prompt hash values for existing projects the next time they are recomputed; downstream drift/resume logic is now stable across Rust/std upgrades, but old persisted hashes will not match the new algorithm until refreshed through normal prompt rewrite/sync flows.
- The workspace still contains the pre-existing untracked `.ralph-burning/` directory, which I left untouched.
